response.justification: The candidate is a highly accomplished senior/staff engineer with nearly two decades of experience, which makes him dramatically overqualified for an entry-level junior position — a significant retention and compensation-mismatch risk. More critically, the role requires strong verbal and written German, while his resume lists German as basic, and his core stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang with only peripheral Java exposure. He meets the degree, agile, and cloud requirements easily, but this is fundamentally the wrong seniority and language profile for this role.
strengths: Deep software engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, and testable architecture across web, mobile, and embedded systems
Computer science degree from ETH Zürich plus hands-on cloud and containerization experience (AWS CDK, Docker, Kubernetes)
Already based in Berlin with long tenure in German tech companies and strong agile team collaboration
concerns: Severely overqualified for an entry-level role, posing high flight risk and likely salary expectations far above junior banding
Only basic German, where the role explicitly requires strong verbal and written German for insurance-sector client work
Java appears only as a listed skill with minor project use; primary expertise is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang rather than Java

key_factors.factor: Strong engineering fundamentals and agile experience
